message:
feat(tools): send_message alias normalization and stricter unique constraint detection

Add normalize_send_message_aliases() to accept common parameter name
variants that LLM tool-callers frequently produce:
- project_key aliases: project, project_slug, human_key
- sender_name aliases: from, from_agent, requester
- message_id alias: id

The normalization is deliberately scoped to send_message/reply_message
only and does not touch parameters belonging to other tools (e.g.,
request_contact's from_agent/to_agent remain untouched).

Improve is_agent_unique_constraint_error() to parse the constraint
message structurally (split on colon, extract column names after the
last dot) instead of substring matching. This prevents false positives
on constraints like "project_id, project_name" which contain both
substrings but refer to a different unique index.

Update the send_message tool description to clarify that broadcast=true
is intentionally rejected (reserved for schema compat only).

Tests:
- normalize_send_message_arguments_does_not_rewrite_non_message_agent_fields
- is_agent_unique_constraint_error handles column-only format and rejects
  near-miss constraints

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
